channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Neymar Faces Uncertainty For Brazil’s World Cup Opener After Calf Injury
item.description: Neymar, the 34-year-old forward from Santos, dey face wahala as e fit miss Brazil first game for 2026 World Cup because e injure him calf. Brazil national team doctor, Rodrigo Lasmar, talk say Neymar ...

item.title: Kenyans react to police communication during Utumishi Girls Academy dormitory fire as parents seek updates on victims
item.description: A tragic fire incident at Utumishi Girls Academy in Gilgil has left the country in shock after a dormitory blaze at around 3am claimed the lives of 16 students and left more than 70 others injured. Th...

item.title: Ruto Defends Fuel Subsidy Policy and Says Transport Strike Was Called Off Voluntarily
item.description: Kenyan President William Ruto has pushed back against claims that his government pressured transport sector leaders to cancel their strike. In remarks at the National Prayer Breakfast, he described th...

item.title: Pentagon Loan to Vulcan Elements Accelerated by White House Intervention Linked to Trump Jr.
item.description: Defense Department records and reporting by ProPublica reveal that a $620 million Pentagon loan to the North Carolina startup Vulcan Elements, linked to Donald Trump Jr., was directly influenced by Wh...

item.title: Couple in Nova Scotia faces charges after infant remains found, to appear in Halifax court for bail
item.description: Authorities in Nova Scotia have charged a local couple following the discovery of the remains of an infant. The couple is scheduled to appear in Halifax court for a bail hearing. Law enforcement has n...

item.title: Augustus Kyalo Muli appointed EACCIA Committee Chair for Membership and Partnerships
item.description: The East African Chamber of Commerce, Industry, and Agriculture (EACCIA) has appointed Kenyan businessman Augustus Kyalo Muli as the Chairperson of its Committee on Membership and Partnerships. The ap...

item.title: Durban model Neetasha Singh represents South Africa on Cannes Film Festival red carpet
item.description: Phoenix-born Durban model and businesswoman Neetasha Singh recently attended the 79th Cannes Film Festival in France, describing the experience as life-changing. Invited by Women in Film and Televisio...

item.title: US Justice Department opens criminal inquiry into E. Jean Carroll over alleged perjury in Trump-related civil cases
item.description: The US Department of Justice has reportedly opened a criminal investigation into E. Jean Carroll, the former magazine columnist who accused Donald Trump of sexual assault. According to reporting cited...

item.title: Gina Rinehart acquires stake in Southern Cross Media amid growing billionaire influence in Australian media
item.description: The article examines reports that mining magnate Gina Rinehart has backed a stake in Southern Cross Media, the owner of the Seven Network, marking her deeper involvement in Australia’s media landscape...

item.title: Kenyan family conducts funeral for Rachel Wandeto in Kerugoya despite missing body and ongoing burial permit dispute
item.description: The Wandeto family in Kerugoya faced an emotionally charged moment as they proceeded with a burial ceremony for Rachel Wandeto despite not having her body. The hearse arrived at the burial site empty,...

item.title: General Compute bets on inference-focused AI infrastructure using SambaNova chips
item.description: The article explores how the surging demand for AI compute, especially for inference workloads, is reshaping the infrastructure landscape and creating opportunities for new players. A startup called G...

item.title: Alpine teams up with Gucci as title sponsor for F1 2027 season
item.description: In a major development for Formula One, Italian fashion brand Gucci will become the title sponsor of Alpine from the 2027 season, creating the Gucci Racing Alpine Formula One Team. The deal, reportedl...

item.title: Youth in Musoma debate gender roles, unemployment, and work responsibility
item.description: In Musoma, Mara Region, a youth dialogue organised by The Chanzo and Twaweza East Africa brought out strong views on how young men and women see work and responsibility. During the discussion, many yo...

item.title: Japan pledges support for Philippines’ strategic petroleum reserve amid energy crisis
item.description: The Japanese government has committed to supporting the Philippines in developing its strategic petroleum reserve program as the country faces potential energy challenges due to ongoing tensions in th...

item.title: Governor Fubara pledges continued peace, unity and inclusive development in Rivers State anniversary message
item.description: Governor Siminalayi Fubara of Rivers State has restated his administration’s strong commitment to maintaining peace, unity, security, and inclusive development as the state marked its 59th anniversary...

item.title: Baguio court sentences woman to life imprisonment for operating drug den and possession of shabu
item.description: A regional trial court in Baguio City has sentenced a woman to life imprisonment after finding her guilty of violating Republic Act 9165, also known as the Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act of 2002. T...

item.title: Ishaq Dar urges Pakistani diaspora in US to invest in Pakistan amid economic recovery claims
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has urged overseas Pakistanis, particularly the Pakistani-American community, to “honour their roots” by investing in Pakistan and contributing to ...

item.title: Nigeria Boxing Federation targets medal haul at 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow
item.description: The Nigeria Boxing Federation (NBF) has set an ambitious target of winning 10 medals, including six gold, at the 2026 Commonwealth Games scheduled to hold in Glasgow, Scotland. The federation says the...

item.title: Italy Issues Red Alert As Heatwave Hits Rome, Florence, Bologna, Brescia, And Turin
item.description: Italy's health ministry don declare red heatwave alert for Rome, Florence, Bologna, Brescia, and Turin as unusual heat dey hit parts of Europe. This na the first red alert of the year for Italy, and e...

item.title: Robert Reich argues Trump’s recent behavior and health concerns raise questions about his fitness for office
item.description: In this opinion article, former U.S. Labor Secretary and public policy professor Robert Reich argues that President Donald Trump’s recent public behavior and repeated medical visits have intensified c...
